headlines.title: Rwanda-Backed Rebels Overrun Troops and Enter Goma Displacing Over One Million
headlines.summary: Rebels overran Congolese troops entering Goma, sparking gunfire and shelling; U.N. reports over 1 million displaced inside the aid hub city. Threatens humanitarian crisis; impacts Central African stability and refugee flows.

headlines.title: Israel Allows Displaced Palestinians to Cross Zone into Northern Gaza After Hostage Deal
headlines.summary: Israel permits Gazans to return on foot across bisecting military zone after hostage release deadlock breaks. Affects hundreds of thousands of displaced; signals possible shift in enclave control dynamics.

headlines.title: U.S. Permits Russian Oil Tanker to Reach Cuba Delivering Crude Amid Blockade
headlines.summary: Russian tanker carrying crude nears Cuban waters; Trump states no issue with delivery despite U.S. blockade, easing island's energy shortages. Affects Cuba's economy; underscores evolving U.S. stance on Russia ties.

framings.angle: Highlights tanker reaching destination by Monday as lifeline amid U.S. pressure
Reports tanker entering waters as Trump softens de facto blockade rhetoric
Quotes Trump expressing no problem with tanker providing relief to Cuba

headlines.title: Floods Kill 110 Across 30 Counties in Kenya as Rains Continue
headlines.summary: Heavy rains raise flood death toll to 110 pounding multiple regions; affects thousands displaced. Government coordinates relief; heightens disaster risks in East Africa.

headlines.title: SpaceX Launches Four Astronauts to Retrieve NASA Crew Stuck on ISS Nine Months
headlines.summary: Crew-10 including two NASA astronauts lifts off Friday from Kennedy Center to relieve Butch Wilmore and Suni Williams. Docks Saturday; enables stuck pair's return, advances station operations.

headlines.title: China Resumes Regular Direct Flights to Pyongyang After Six Years
headlines.summary: Air China restarts Beijing-Pyongyang Boeing 737 services Monday; ambassador welcomes passengers. Warms bilateral ties post-train restoration; boosts North Korea logistics and diplomacy.

headlines.title: China's Vanke Reports Huge Losses Signaling Widening Property Crisis
headlines.summary: Leading developer Vanke posts massive losses amid sector troubles; raises bailout questions. Affects investors and economy; tests Beijing's intervention limits.

headlines.title: Japan Lower House Passes 8.56 Trillion Yen Emergency Stopgap Budget
headlines.summary: Lawmakers approve first temporary measure in 11 years across parties. Funds government operations; averts fiscal shortfall impacting economy.

headlines.title: UN Votes Transatlantic Slave Trade Gravest Crime Boosting Reparations Push
headlines.summary: Resolution passes despite opposition declaring slavery gravest crime; energizes African Union efforts. Affects global reparations debates; shapes historical accountability.

headlines.title: NATO Mounts First Coordinated Response to Suspected Baltic Sea Cable Sabotage
headlines.summary: Alliance launches initial action after another deep-sea cable severance amid sabotage fears. Protects critical infrastructure; escalates hybrid threat concerns in Europe.
headlines.previousContext: Alliance mounts first coordinated response after another cable severed in sabotage campaign

headlines.title: Israeli Military Confirms Strike Kills Three Lebanese Journalists Including Al Manar Reporter
headlines.summary: Broadcasters report deaths in southern Lebanon; Israel verifies killing Ali Shoeib from Hezbollah-linked TV. Raises media safety issues; fuels regional tensions.
headlines.previousContext: Israeli military confirms killing Ali Shoeib from Al Manar TV

headlines.title: Iranian Attacks Injure Workers at Major Aluminum Sites in UAE and Bahrain
headlines.summary: Strikes hit industrial facilities across Gulf continuing campaign; several injured. Disrupts production; heightens energy security risks in region.
headlines.previousContext: Attacks continue targeting aluminum plants with injuries reported
